title: ranunculus eschscholtzii subsp. trisectus
intro: Ranunculus eschscholtzii subsp. trisectus, also known as trisect buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus eschscholtzii subsp. trisectus are said to include pain relief, wound healing, and antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
